observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a. Direct the Inventory Committee to fast-track the completion of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE following the procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006;

We recommended that management:

c. Pending cleansing of PPE account, require the municipal accountant and the GSO to maintain separate ledger and property cards and PAR for acquisitions of PPE in C.Y. 2024 onwards and conduct regular physical count.
observations.reason: The recommendation is only partially implemented. Only PARs were prepared by the Supply Officer; property ledger cards are incomplete while no property cards were prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a.       Enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan, spearheaded by the Municipal Development Council, to allocate sufficient budget for the completion of the project, and
observations.reason: The Provincial Government of Misamis Occidental has already commenced implementation of their project for the continuation of the LGU Lopez Jaena's upgrading of the water system. LGU Lopez Jaena is constrained to wait until Province's project is finished before they can allocate funds for the completion of the entire project up to the operational state of the project.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a.       Direct the Local Chief Executive to spearhead the settlement of road right of way for the access road to the Sanitary Landfill;
observations.reason: Negotiation is on-going with the private land-owners.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

b.       Enjoin the Sanggunian Bayan to provide funding requirements for just compensation to the private lot owners as may be agreed upon, if the option of donation will not prosper; and
observations.reason: Negotiation is on-going with the private land-owners.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive through the Human Resource Management Office to publish the vacancy of the Municipal Assessor position and accordingly appoint a qualified applicant.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to implement the recommendation in CY 2025

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayers' index card and furnish the Municipal Accountant the required duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year.
observations.reason: Not all Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayers' index cards were updated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a.       Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the inventory balances and submit the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the Audit Team; and
observations.reason: Due to volume of work, the Municipal Accountant has not yet taken action to address the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

b.       Enjoin the Municipal Development Council through the MPDO to:

2)     Ensure that disclosures in the Full Disclosure Policy (FDP) Portal of the DILG reflect the actual status of the development projects.
observations.reason: Per verification in the FDP Portal of the DILG, the "% of completion" column was still based on the cost incurred, not on the physical accomplishment of the project

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a.       Direct the Bids and Awards Committee through the BAC Secretariat to ensure that the Notice of Award, Purchase Order/Contract of Agreement, and Notice to Proceed, for future procurement through any of the alternative modes, are posted in the PhilGEPS website as provided under Section 54.3 of IRR of RA No. 9184
observations.reason: TheBids and Awards Committee through the BAC Secretariat still did not practice posting in the PhilGEPS website the Notice of Award, Purchase Order/Contract of Agreement, and Notice to Proceed from procurement through alternative modes.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that management instruct the Municipal Assessor to secure and commence the processing of the TCT of all the land properties, and seek budgetary allocations for the pertinent cost thereof.
observations.reason: Since the conduct of exit conference for CY 2023 AAR, no progress has been noted since the Municipal Assessor designate has been awaiting  for allocation of funds to proceed with titling process of the other land properties of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to retrieve all supporting documents for unpaid liabilities of the Municipality that may be in their custody and validate the documents that may be gathered;
Send notices to the creditors affected for possible claim, and
If still not claimed within reasonable period of time, cause reversion to the Unappropriated Surplus pursuant to Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 for fair presentation of the account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to evaluate the purpose and status of the dormant bank accounts and, if deemed no longer necessary, close these accounts and return the balances to their respective source agencies or original funding sources.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require its Local Development Council, in consultation with the Local Finance Committee, to update yearly its Local Development Investment Plan (LDIP) and ensure that all projects in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) as well as the subsequent reprogramming are among the listed projects in the LDIP.

We recommended that the concerned Department Heads of the Municipality where various LGU programs, projects and activities are spearheaded, to strictly comply the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 where the monitoring of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year contain details as to project name, location, total cost, date started, extensions, target and actual completion dates, project status and total cost incurred to date, be prepared and be provided to the Audit Team.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, in the future similar projects, prepare Plans and Program of Work to the extent that the projects are usable and operational.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Establish and strengthen the gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system by issuing the necessary Executive or Administrative Order and formally constituting the GAD M&E Team;
Conduct regular assessment of institutional mechanisms for gender mainstreaming to monitor the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD implementation; and
Prepare and ensure the timely submission of the annual LGU Status Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming to the DILG Regional Office.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive as Co-chairman of the Local School Board (LSB) to:
Instruct the LSB Secretary to furnish, with proof of receipt, the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the Local Treasurer a copy of the duly approved SEF Budget not later than seven (7) days after its approval;
Direct the LSB Secretary to submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF utilization to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG; and
Instruct the focal person for the DILG Full Disclosure Policy to ensure that the SEF utilization reports are posted in at least three (3) conspicuous public places not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter and in the FDP Portal within the quarter set in the Joint Circular, and to record the date of posting.

We recommended that management:

b. Designate a full-time General Services Officer who will be responsible for the preservation, maintenance, and safekeeping of the PPEs of the LGU; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has designated a Supply Officer who will be responsible for the preservation, maintenance, and safekeeping of the PPEs of the LGU

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a.       Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submit the said schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office;
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had submitted the Schedule of Unreleased Checks to the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

b.       Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts every year-end for the total amount of unreleased checks, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has drawn the necessary adjusting journal entry.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a.     Direct the MEED Officer to submit proposal for the procurement and installation of computerized billing and collection system for the MEEDO;
observations.managementAction: The MEED Officer had a verbal representation with the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan approved the proposed budget and duly incorporated in the CY 2024 annual budget

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

b.      Enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan to support funding initiatives based on the submitted proposal of the MEED Officer;
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan passed SB Resolution No 71-2024 approving Ordinance No 6 series of 2024 for Supplemental Budget allocating P250,000.00. for the Water Bill Collection System

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

c.     Direct the MEED Officer to submit Report to the Municipal Accountant on the Lopez Jaena Water System Receivables as of every month end as basis for the recording;
observations.managementAction: The MEED Officer has submitted the Report on the Lopez Jaena Water System Receivables to the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

d.     Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the receivables from Lopez Jaena Water System subscribers and maintain subsidiary ledger for every account; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has recorded the receivables from the  Lopez Jaena Water System subscribers and has maintained subsidiary ledger for every account

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

e.     For the subsequent issuance of Financial Statements, require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements relevant information regarding the Lopez Jaena Water System receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements relevant information regarding the Lopez Jaena Water System receivables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a.     Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw correcting entries treating the errors on the transfer of unspent prior year LDRRMF to the Trust Fund; and
observations.managementAction: The error self-corrected upon actual transfer of the fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

b.    Direct the Municipal Accountant to revert the beyond five-year unutilized DRRMF balances amounting P 95,978 to the Unappropriated Surplus.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has reverted the fund to the general fund and the Sangguniang Bayan has passed a supplemental budget treating the reverted fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management initiate the increase in the amount of fidelity bond proportionate to the actual accountabilities of the Accountable Officer to ensure that indemnification from the Fidelity Fund due to misuse of funds and other losses shall be enough to cover the said loss.
observations.managementAction: The amount of fidelity bonds of the Collectors has been increased from P30,000 to P75,000 - P100,000.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

b.       Henceforth, require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Program of Works (POW) on infrastructure programs prior to the approval of the budget to improve implementation effectiveness and efficiency.
observations.managementAction: Program of Works (POW) were already prepared for infrastructure projects prior to the approval of the CY 2025 Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

a.       Direct the Municipal Engineer to suspend the further construction of the Sanitary Landfill until issuance of the Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC); and
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer immediately suspended further construction of the Sanitary Landfill prior to the issuance of the Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

b.       Direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to spearhead the process of securing ECC at the DENR-Environmental Management Bureau.
observations.managementAction: The DENR-EMB Region 10 has already issued the Environmental Compliance Certificate, ECC-OL-R10-2024-0286 dated October 23, 2024

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

c.       Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to conduct better preliminary detailed engineering to address right-of-way issues prior to project implementation, hence projects with right-of-way issues should not be implemented and funded until resolved.
observations.managementAction: For projects implemented since receipt of the audit observation memorandum, settlement of right-of-way issues was already considered before commencement of the project implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a.       Transfer fund equivalent to P76,211.97 to the Special Trust Fund out of the   General Fund to compensate the funds used from unrelated DRRM purposes; and
observations.managementAction: The fund was already replenished per voucher no. 100-2024-08-2034 dated August 30, 2024

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

b.    Strictly observe Section 5 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was compliant as to charging of expenditures against the DRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a.       Require the JO workers to quantify daily accomplishments in the reports; and
observations.managementAction: A more detailed accomplishment reports were already required from the job order personnel as a requirement for payment of their wages.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

b.       Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to evaluate the delegated tasks to job order workers based on their accomplishments and assess their effectiveness and efficiency to determine reasonableness of their service.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO had conducted an evaluation on the reasonableness of the job order personnel's service based on their actual accomplishments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enjoin the Local School Board to incorporate ECCD Programs in the PPAs appropriated for Special Education Fund pursuant to Section 7(b) of RA No. 10410.
observations.managementAction: The School Board Committee has passed Resolution No. 1 series of 2024 where an ECCD Program for School Supplies amounting to P50,000 was allocated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the GAD Focal Point System to attach the documents required in paragraph 5 of Section 4.C.8 of JMC 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the GAD PPAs implemented during the year.
observations.managementAction: The GAD TWG Focal has already attached the necessary supporting documents in the GAD Accomplishment Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

b.       Enjoin the Supply Officer acting as the GSO to prepare and maintain Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant as the latter’s basis for the recording of expenditures and conduct periodic reconciliation of accounting records with actual inventories on hand through the conduct semestral physical count and preparation of the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI)
observations.managementAction: The Supply Officer has prepared, maintained, and submitted the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a.       Instruct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that the Annual Procurement Plan of the LGU includes all items subject to procurement by the LGU including infrastructure projects; and
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat has already prepared the Annual Procurement Plan which incorporates all procurement items of the LGU including infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

b.       Enjoin the Municipal Development Council through the MPDO to:

1)     Ensure that development projects are well-planned and are procurement-and-implementation-ready; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has considered right of way issues prior to implementation of the project and that Programs of Works (POW) were prepared prior to the approval of the Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the officials and employees to immediately refund the excessive registration fee of P2,000 per participant and submit justification on their non-attendance on August 14, 2022 session of the seminar/workshop.
observations.managementAction: All affected have already refunded the excessive registration fee of P2,000 per participant.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management direct the designated special implementing unit spearheaded by the OIC-Municipal Agriculturist to communicate in writing of any progress, changes, delay or any concern relating to the complete and effective implementation of the project to the Undersecretary of Livestock and the DA Regional Field Office, in accordance with the MOA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist has already sent via courier a written communication to the Undersecretary of Livestock and the Department of Agriculture Regional Field Office informing them of the completion of the project enclosed with the supporting documents evidencing implementation of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayers' index card and furnish the Municipal Accountant the required duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasury Office personnel are currently updating the RPTAR.
observations.reason: Due to thousands of real property taxpayers, it would take considerable time to complete its updating.
The Municipal Accountant has not prepared adjustment due to non-preparation of the RPCI.

observations.managementAction: SSMI has been prepared by the Supply Officer.
observations.reason: However, reconciliation of records was not made due to non-conduct of semestral physical count of inventories.

observations.managementAction: Though APP for the Infra was prepared, it was not consolidated with goods and consulting services, if any.
observations.reason: No consolidated APP was prepared.
There are projects in CY 2023 that were not yet implemented.
Per verification in the DILG Portal, not all entries are filled out.
The BAC Secretariat was not able to post the documents in the PhilGEPS since the personnel in charge for the posting in the PhilGEPS was not made aware of.
The Management has not yet refunded the excess registration fee.

observations.managementAction: Lots 2184 and 3800 are already titled. Lots 2996, 1880, and 3031 are not yet surveyed pending fund allocation. Lot 1817 is still in the process of cancellation of its Mortgage in the PDIC.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management Communicate in writing of any progress, changes, delay or any concern relating to the complete and effective implementation of the project to the Undersecretary of Livestock and the DA Regional Field Office, in accordance with the MOA.
observations.reason: The management has not submitted to the Audit Team any written communication to the DA Regional Office informing their office of the progress of the project.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
Direct the Inventory Committee to fast-track the completion of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE following the procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006;
Designate a full-time General Services Officer who will be responsible for the preservation, maintenance, and safekeeping of the PPEs of the LGU; and
Pending cleansing of PPE account, require the municipal accountant and the GSO to maintain separate ledger and property cards and PAR for acquisitions of PPE in C.Y. 2024 onwards and conduct regular physical count.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submit the said schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts every year-end for the total amount of unreleased checks, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw correcting entries treating the errors on the transfer of unspent prior year LDRRMF to the Trust Fund; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to revert the beyond five-year unutilized DRRMF balances amounting P 95,978 to the Unappropriated Surplus.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the timeline of submission of BRS to the Audit Team as laid under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant endeavored to prepare and submit BRS on time. She had prepared all BRS, except for the General Fund, up to December 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

observations.managementAction: The management has constituted the Inventory Committee.
observations.reason: This audit finding is reiterated with modification on this years Audit Report.
This audit finding is reiterated with modification on this years Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the PPE in accordance with the criteria set under COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 and prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjusting entries to effect the dropping of the PPE costing below P50,000 and provide a copy of the JEV to the designated GSO together with the schedule or list of affected PPE for appropriate action in accordance with the cited Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had adjusted the affected accounts dropping all the uncapitalizable assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to append the Delivery Receipts (DR) and Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIR) for the subject transactions; otherwise, draw a reversing or correcting entry.  Henceforth, ensure that items recorded in the Accounts Payable are supported with DR and AIR.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has appended the required documents for the subject transactions. Also, she ensured that subsequent AP documents are supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: b.Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw correcting entries treating the under-recording of RPT/SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has drawn necessary adjustments to correct its RPT/SET Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management:

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to present the correction of prior period adjustments/errors by restating the comparative amounts for the period(s) presented in which adjustments/errors occurred, whenever practicable, and ensure adequate disclosures thereof in compliance with IPSAS 3.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant disclosed the prior period adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to remit to the source agencies the amount P415,412.02 and to the Bureau of the Treasury P231,562.22 and draw necessary journal entry voucher treating the return.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has transferred to the various source agencies the unexpended balances of the funds held in trust.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management:

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has posted the Certified List of RPT Delinquent at the Main Entrance of the Municipal Hall and Barangay Hall.

The Local Government devised strategies like raffle promo program for all taxpayers, incentives to top 5 barangays who garnered highest RPT collections, and conducted the Tok-Tok Buhis with the Provincial Treasurer Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management:
We recommended that management:

The Municipality registered 88% utilization rate on its current year mitigation fund of DRRMF.

observations.reason: Per post audit of disbursement vouchers, there are still unrelated expenditures charged against the LDRRM Fund. Thus, reiterated on this years Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the prospective suppliers to indicate brand names together with their relevant specifications during the filling out of the Requests for Quotations (RFQs) and ensure that brand names together with their complete detailed item specifications to be procured from the winning supplier are indicated in the Purchase Orders in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.managementAction: The Management has already implemented purchase order where items are already described with particularity specifically on the brand names.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Engineer to include as a pay item the Project Billboard in the Approved Budget for the Contract and require the contractor to install project billboards at the project sites containing complete project information as soon as the award has been made in accordance with COA Circular 2013-004. Should the contractor fail to install project billboard, deduct its cost from the billing if the same is a pay item.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office has included Project Signboards as pay item.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management:

RATA payments are all supported with complete documentation.
The Municipal Accountant recorded all RATA payments to their appropriate accounts.
The Municipal Accountant has ceased the grant of RETE to Municipal Engineer and Municipal DRRMO who were then not entitled for the RATA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management:

observations.managementAction: The Management has already observed the provision for the ceiling amounts for registration fees.
The Management has implemented and observed the guidelines for travel of JO/COS personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Accountant to ensure complete documentation on the grant of CNA Incentives.
observations.managementAction: The LGU no longer granted CNA to its employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System, in coordination with the Local Planning and Development Office, (a) create a GAD database to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data, as basis in GAD planning, programming and policy-making; and (b) timely submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction: The GAD FPS created a GAD database and submitted GAD Accomplishment Report on time.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal DRRMO has complied the minimum standards. In fact, they have been awarded during the Gawad Kalasag by the Office of the Civil Defense.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendations that management enforce control activities, specifically by:

observations.managementAction: The GSO has implemented post-inspection for every lease of the heavy equipment.
The LGU has now practiced the execution of Lease Contract.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:

1. Require the BSWA to apply for registration in the municipality;
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has passed Municipal Ordinance No. 4-2022, granting authority to the Barangay Water System Association (BAWASA) to use existing water assets of the Municipality.
The Municipality has passed Municipal Ordinance No. 4-2022, granting authority to the Barangay Water System Association (BAWASA) to use existing water assets of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office prepare a working paper describing the assets recorded in the PPE account with an acquisition cost of less than P15,000.00, including the supporting documents such as the Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) of said assets and other relevant data.  Draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) in order to make an adjusting entry to remove the same from the account “Property, Plant, and Equipment”. All these will be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification; and
Subsequent purchases of equipment and other items with acquisition cost of less than P15,000.00 shall  be recorded as inventory upon purchase and expense upon issuance with the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) to establish accountability over assets.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that management:
Instruct the Municipal Assessor to secure and commence the processing of the TCT of all the land properties, and seek budgetary allocations for the pertinent cost thereof; and
Direct the Municipal Assessor to provide copies of TCT to the Municipal Accountant, as basis for the latter to establish a subsidiary list and as basis to elect the revaluation model in accounting for Land as prescribed under IPSAS 17 using latest available Fair Market Value.

We have recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to review the details comprising the Due to LGU account and reconcile with the records of the identified source agency.  If the balances are determined to be valid payables:
Return the funds to source agency, or
Request in writing to the Source Agency for the use of the unexpended balances for other programs of the Municipality and the transfer of the unexpended balance from the Trust Fund to the General Fund.
After reconciliation, effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the account balance.

We have recommended that management direct the designated special implementing unit spearheaded by the OIC-Municipal Agriculturist to properly monitor and implement the project and comply with the provisions of the MOA and the project proposal duly approved by the DA; and
Communicate in writing of any progress, changes, delay or any concern relating to the complete and effective implementation of the project to the Undersecretary of Livestock and the DA Regional Field Office, in accordance with the MOA.

We have recommended that management direct all concerned officials to properly and completely accomplish relevant procurement documents such as PR, RFQ and PO and provide the necessary and relevant specifications of the items to be procured in clear and unambiguous terms for all future procurement projects to serve as reference during inspection and acceptance of deliveries by management.

We have recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to process disbursement vouchers on advance payment to contractors for future projects only upon the submission to and acceptance by the procuring entity of irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission.

We have recommended that management direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to require the contractors to submit the lacking requirements for future infrastructure projects as a pre-requisite prior to payment of final billings pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001.

We have recommended that management streamline and document the donation policy and process thru the issuance of an Executive Order or a Memorandum, which among others, would contain the employees authorized to receive donations and their reportorial responsibilities.

We have recommended that management instruct the MDRRMO thru coordination with concerned municipal officials to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of donations received and distributed by the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO, RHU and MO already submitted and complied the complete list and account for the receipt and distribution of donations in-kind to COA
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:

1.Organize a validation team who will work in close coordination with barangay officials concerned and the DSWD to conduct verification on the eligibility of the aforementioned beneficiaries based on the filled-up SAC; 

2.Direct the validation team to submit to the audit team a report/status of their verification; and 

3.Confer with DSWD-Regional Office No. 10 on the results of the revalidation and obtain a written advice as to the course of action if recipients were declared to be ineligible.
observations.managementAction:
Executed Executive Order No. 10 Series of 2021 dated March 8, 2021 and fully validated the eligibility of the aforementioned beneficiaries.

Verification Status Report submitted to COA dated March 16, 2021 through the Private Secretary of the Municipal Mayor.

Obtained a written advice from the DSWD Regional Office No. X relative to the ineligibility clause dated March 23, 2021.
observations.reason:
Fully Implemented

The concerned beneficiaries already refunded the amounts received.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the BAC to observe strict compliance on the mandatory posting of the procurement data for all contracts awarded under Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.
observations.managementAction: The recommendations were already complied.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that management:

1.Instruct the Municipal Assessor to secure and commence the processing of the TCT of all the land properties, and seek budgetary allocations for the pertinent cost thereof; and

2.Direct the Municipal Assessor to provide copies of TCT to the Municipal Accountant, as basis for the latter to establish a subsidiary list and as basis to elect the revaluation model in accounting for Land as prescribed under IPSAS 17 using latest available Fair Market Value.
observations.managementAction:
The registration of  some land properties have commenced

Out of the 10 untitled real properties; 1 was titled, 6 were already surveyed and 3 were not yet surveyed.

The movement of the DENR personnel has been limited due to the strict implementation of various community quarantine measures that the Government imposed due to COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2021 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:

1.Require the Accounting Office to record first the purchases of drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies as inventory; 

2.Require the RHU Inventory In-Charge to submit the monthly report of issuances to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses for inventories consumed or distributed; and

3.Direct the RHU Inventory In-Charge to prepare and update the stock cards regularly and reconcile it with the results of physical inventory count at least on a semestral basis.
observations.managementAction:

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:

1.	Direct the HRMO to execute contract for employees hired under JO to include the following:

a.Statement of duties and responsibilities as a means by which their performance can be measured; and

b.Attestation on the part of the person to be hired that he is not related within fourth degree of consanguinity or affinity with the contracting officer and the appointing authority of the hiring agency.

2.	Direct all Job Order workers to attach their actual accomplishment reports in their DTRs to be reviewed and verified by their respective immediate supervisor; and

3.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments to job order workers are supported with their accomplishment reports for actual services rendered aside from the duly approved DTRs.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management provide legal basis citing that the said payment of honoraria to the MBOC shall be sourced from the LGU fund and submit a certificate of non-payment of honoraria from the COMELEC Central Office Accountant.  Otherwise, bill the COMELEC for the total election expenses incurred during the May 13, 2019 National and Local Elections in accordance with Section 10 of the Omnibus Election Code.

Revised recommendation:
Pursuant to COMELEC Minute Resolution No. 19-1054, submit justification that the honoraria granted to the Municipal Board of Canvassers during the May 13, 2019 National and Local Elections is not similar to the honorarium or allowance given by the COMELEC, otherwise the payment shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 7 | Already submitted a legal basis to support the claim.
observations.reason:
COMELEC Minute Resolution No. 07-0626 dated March 27, 2007 is superseded by COMELEC Minute Resolution No. 19-1054 dated October 30, 2019, which resolved that the Sangguniang Bayan, Sangguniang Panlungsod,  Sangguniang Panlalawigan of municipalities, cities and provinces, respectively:
May continue to grant an honorarium or allowance to the members of the municipal/city/provincial board of canvassers for the May 13, 2019 National and Local Elections and all subsequent electoral exercises; such honorarium or allowance must not be similar to the honorarium or allowance being given by this Commission; and
May provide in their respective ordinances that of the recipient is already receiving a similar honorarium or allowance for the same service or period, respectively, for which payment is being claimed, the recipient may choose to  receive whichever is higher, but in no case must the recipient receive both amounts.
Hence, the audit team revised the audit recommendation.

We have recommended the following:

a.	The concerned personnel of the accounting office should trace back the prior years accounting records to come up with the details of each program/project recorded under the account Due to NGAs and subsequently, return any unspent/unused balances, including the PDAF/CDF, to the source agency should the MOA requires its return as required under Item 3.2.g.3 of COA Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 29, 2015.

b.	Direct the bookkeeper in charge to set up control systems for the proper accounting on Due to National Government Agencies account by securing MOA and monitor the provisions of its project duration, the submission of status/terminal reports and maintain copies of related documents on file for easy reference.

observations.recommendation:

b. 	The local legislative body shall include in the appropriation ordinance of the municipality the amount necessary for the insurance coverage of LGU properties as recommended by the LCE.

c. 	The Property Officer shall facilitate the application with the GSIS of an insurance coverage of all its properties that the municipality has an insurable interest to insure that any loss or damage to the same will be indemnified by the insurer.
